locked_facts: Σp = constant when net external force F_ext = 0 (conservation of linear momentum)
Newton's second law: F = dp/dt (force equals rate of change of momentum)
Rocket propulsion: thrust = v_exhaust × (dm/dt), momentum of rocket + exhaust is conserved
Recoil of gun: m_bullet × v_bullet = m_gun × v_recoil (total momentum = 0 before firing)
Momentum is a vector — conservation applies independently to each component (x, y, z)
truth_statements: The total momentum of an isolated system (no external forces) remains constant in magnitude and direction
Newton's third law guarantees momentum conservation: internal forces are equal and opposite, so net internal impulse is zero
Impulse equals the change in momentum of a body, and equals the area under the force-time graph
In rocket propulsion, the rocket gains forward momentum equal to the backward momentum of ejected exhaust gases
Momentum conservation is more fundamental than energy conservation — it holds even when KE is not conserved

aha_moment.what_to_show: A gun firing a bullet — before firing, total momentum = 0. After: bullet moves right with large v, gun recoils left with small v. Momentum bars: m_bullet × v_bullet (right) + m_gun × v_recoil (left) = 0. Both bars equal in magnitude, opposite in direction.
aha_moment.visual_emphasis: Total momentum bar stays at zero throughout. Individual momentum bars are equal and opposite. F × Δt on bullet = -F × Δt on gun.
aha_moment.why_this_breaks_misconception: Students see that momentum is created in PAIRS — you cannot create momentum in one direction without creating equal momentum in the opposite direction

simulation_states.label: Setup
simulation_states.description: A gun and bullet system at rest. Total momentum = 0 displayed prominently. Mass of bullet (small) and gun (large) labeled. Momentum bar for system at zero.
simulation_states.emphasis: System is isolated (no external horizontal force). Total initial momentum = 0.

simulation_states.label: Buildup
simulation_states.description: Gun fires. Explosion force acts as internal force (equal and opposite on bullet and gun). Bullet flies right, gun recoils left. Individual momentum bars grow in opposite directions. Force-time impulse shown.
simulation_states.emphasis: Internal forces: F on bullet = -F on gun. Impulse on bullet = -Impulse on gun. Net impulse = 0.

simulation_states.label: Aha
simulation_states.description: Final state: p_bullet (right, large magnitude due to high v) + p_gun (left, same magnitude due to large m) = 0. Total momentum bar still at zero. Numerical proof: m_bullet × v_bullet = m_gun × v_recoil.
simulation_states.emphasis: Total momentum is STILL zero. Momentum was redistributed, not created. m_b v_b = m_g v_g.

simulation_states.label: Explore
simulation_states.description: Interactive: adjust bullet mass, gun mass, and bullet speed. See recoil velocity update via conservation. Switch to rocket mode: continuous exhaust ejection, rocket accelerates. Adjust exhaust velocity and mass flow rate.
simulation_states.emphasis: Heavier gun = less recoil. Faster bullet = more recoil. Rocket: thrust = v_exhaust × dm/dt.

common_misconceptions.belief: A heavier object has more momentum so it always wins in a collision
common_misconceptions.correction: Momentum depends on BOTH mass and velocity: p = mv. A light bullet with very high velocity can have more momentum than a heavy slow object. In collisions, momentum is redistributed, not won or lost.
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Show light fast bullet vs heavy slow block — bullet has more momentum. After collision, momentum transfers.

common_misconceptions.belief: Momentum is not conserved in explosions because energy is added
trigger_phrases: energy add hone se momentum badh gaya
bomb blast mein momentum create hota hai
common_misconceptions.correction: Momentum IS conserved in explosions. The explosion forces are INTERNAL to the system. Chemical energy converts to KE, but total momentum of all fragments equals the initial momentum of the bomb (which is zero if stationary).
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Show bomb at rest exploding: fragments fly in all directions, but vector sum of all momenta = 0

common_misconceptions.belief: Impulse and force are the same thing
common_misconceptions.correction: Impulse J = F × Δt is force multiplied by the time duration. A small force applied for long time can give the same impulse as a large force for short time. Catching a ball gently (small F, large Δt) gives the same Δp as catching it with stiff hands (large F, small Δt).
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Show same ball caught two ways: soft catch (long Δt, small F) vs hard catch (short Δt, large F) — same impulse Δp
